description: Type 2 Diabetes management with insulin therapy adjustment
system_instruction: Focus on comprehensive diabetes management, insulin titration, and complication screening in a patient with poorly controlled diabetes.

case_metadata.title: Uncontrolled Type 2 Diabetes with Insulin Adjustment

patient_persona.name: Carlos Rodriguez
patient_persona.chief_complaint: High blood sugar readings and frequent urination
patient_persona.background_story: Diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes 8 years ago, poor glycemic control despite oral medications

initial_prompt: My blood sugar has been running really high lately. I'm checking it at home and it's often over 250. I'm also getting up to urinate many times at night.

clinical_dossier.hidden_diagnosis: Uncontrolled Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus requiring insulin intensification

social_history.diet_and_exercise: High carbohydrate diet, sedentary job, no regular exercise
social_history.living_situation: Lives with wife and teenage children

physical_exam_findings.general: Obese male in no acute distress
physical_exam_findings.eyes: Non-proliferative diabetic retinopathy on fundoscopic exam
physical_exam_findings.feet: Decreased vibratory sensation bilaterally, intact pulses
physical_exam_findings.neurological: Decreased monofilament sensation in both feet

end_session.patient_response: I understand the insulin adjustment plan. I'll monitor my blood sugar and follow up as scheduled.

critical_issue.response: Patient reports symptoms of hypoglycemia. Immediate assessment and glucose administration required.

evaluation_criteria.History_Taking: Comprehensive assessment of glycemic control, adherence, and complications
evaluation_criteria.Medication_Management: Appropriate insulin initiation and titration strategy
evaluation_criteria.Patient_Education: Effective counseling on lifestyle modifications and glucose monitoring
evaluation_criteria.Follow_up_Planning: Establishment of appropriate monitoring schedule and goals
evaluation_criteria.Multidisciplinary_Coordination: Referral to diabetes education, nutrition, and ophthalmology

description: Community-acquired pneumonia management in an elderly patient with multiple comorbidities
system_instruction: Focus on diagnosis, antibiotic selection, and management of pneumonia in an elderly patient with comorbidities.

patient_persona.name: Helen Baker
patient_persona.chief_complaint: Fever, cough, and shortness of breath for 4 days
patient_persona.background_story: Lives alone, became progressively more short of breath and confused over past 2 days

initial_prompt: I've had this cough for a few days and it's getting worse. I feel feverish and can't catch my breath just walking to the bathroom.

history_of_presenting_illness.character: Productive cough with green sputum, pleuritic chest pain

social_history.living_situation: Lives alone in apartment, daughter checks in daily

physical_exam_findings.general: Elderly female, appears confused, in respiratory distress
physical_exam_findings.chest: Decreased breath sounds right lower lobe, crackles, bronchial breath sounds

end_session.patient_response: Thank you for explaining everything. I understand I need to be admitted.

critical_issue.response: Patient meets SIRS criteria. Immediate sepsis protocol activation required.

evaluation_criteria.Diagnostic_Assessment: Appropriate clinical evaluation and diagnostic testing for pneumonia
evaluation_criteria.Severity_Assessment: Proper use of CURB-65 or similar severity scoring
evaluation_criteria.Antibiotic_Selection: Appropriate empiric antibiotic therapy considering allergies and comorbidities
evaluation_criteria.Supportive_Care: Appropriate oxygen therapy, hydration, and monitoring
evaluation_criteria.Geriatric_Considerations: Assessment of delirium, fall risk, and functional status
evaluation_criteria.Discharge_Planning: Appropriate criteria for discharge and follow-up arrangement
